evals.prompt: Publish today's personalized edition. The saved context contains one love reaction, but a second reaction arrives after research and before the POST. Finish safely without losing either preference signal.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the saved snapshot to shape the draft, receives a stale-context rejection, leaves the old edition live, captures new context, regenerates under a new id, and verifies the final publication.
expectations: Uses edition:context and edition:prepare instead of hand-writing generation fields
Does not patch contextVersion, contextRevision, or reactions merely to make the POST pass
Does not consume the late reaction with content that ignored it
Verifies the final live reader and post-publication context

evals.prompt: Create a daily paper when only five candidate items are available: two primary sources agree, two reports conflict on the event date, and one attractive image has unclear reuse provenance.
evals.expected_output: The edition uses only supportable claims, preserves or reports meaningful uncertainty, omits the unverified image, combines thin subjects, and publishes a smaller coherent newspaper rather than filler.
expectations: Drops or qualifies source-conflicted claims instead of choosing the convenient date
Omits the image whose provenance or usage basis cannot be verified
Combines thin sections and does not create one story per page
Maps every printed paragraph to detailed support and source ids

evals.prompt: Redesign an edition with nine verified stories across technology, culture, and sports so it feels like a paper newspaper on desktop and remains composed at exactly 560px and on a phone.
evals.expected_output: The agent applies the base-design print grammar, creates subject pages with different silhouettes and irregular hierarchy, balances independent lanes so copy does not strand large cavities, preserves the reader-selected press palette, keeps article controls inside each package, and verifies all three target widths in a real browser.
expectations: Reads references/base-design.md before composing pages
Uses layout-only page HTML with one empty article placement per story
Creates subject pages rather than one page per article
Shows only love and less controls inside articles and verifies desktop, 560px, and phone
Uses semantic palette variables and lets the trusted reader apply the selected press theme
Rejects alignment-created cavities or avoidable lane-ending differences larger than four lines of neighboring body copy
Verifies edge hover and focus labels, touch affordances, directional motion, and reduced motion

evals.prompt: The edition POST returns 201, but production verification finds that one article cannot open and the current edition changed while recovery was being considered. Report the correct result and protect the reader.
evals.expected_output: The agent does not call the POST a success, attempts only the guarded direct-predecessor restore, refuses to overwrite a newer current edition on 409, and reports the failed id plus the actual current state.
expectations: Treats browser and context verification as required after the POST
Uses edition:restore with both direct predecessor and expected current ids
Does not force restoration after the current edition changes
Reports failure and the evidence-backed current state without claiming completion

evals.prompt: The owner dislikes one long technical article and asks whether the system should permanently exclude AI security and infer their job role from that click.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats the click as one weak signal about non-sensitive topic, format, depth, and style metadata; it preserves discovery and does not infer identity or create a permanent exclusion without an explicit profile change.
expectations: Treats one less reaction as weak evidence
Learns only from the closed non-sensitive preferenceTags and editorial dimensions
Does not infer occupation or another sensitive identity trait
Does not turn a dislike into a profile exclusion

evals.prompt: Yesterday's nine-story edition used an 8/4 lead with a bottom strip. Today's edition also has nine verified stories, but their importance, text lengths, relationships, and image evidence differ. Publish without turning the base design into a fixed template.
evals.expected_output: The agent inventories today's content, records the current live silhouettes, derives new page-specific composition from editorial relationships, changes at least two structural dimensions where content permits, and preserves only the stable reader and safety contract.
expectations: Creates a content-derived adaptive layout brief before page HTML or CSS
Uses the edition template only for the data contract and does not copy its CSS or composition
Compares with the current live edition and changes at least two structural dimensions or records an evidence-based exception
Keeps variation editorial rather than random while preserving the stable reader contract

evals.prompt: I installed the Codex Reporter plugin and opened a new Codex task in an empty project. I have no repository checkout, deployed site, token, or environment file. Create my paper, discuss my preferences with me, publish an initial edition, then update it every day at 00:00 Asia/Taipei without depending on this chat remaining open.
evals.expected_output: The agent bootstraps the bundled application into the empty project, creates an isolated reader-owned Sites/D1/R2 deployment and private runtime credential without asking for infrastructure configuration, asks only the required profile questions, publishes and verifies the first edition, then creates exactly one active project automation with the canonical unattended prompt.
expectations: Uses the bundled bootstrap script and never asks the reader to clone the repository, run pnpm, or enter environment variables
Creates a new Sites project and never reuses the example project id, URL, credentials, profile, reactions, or editions
Asks one required profile question at a time and does not invent tracking or storage settings
Saves the confirmed profile and verifies an initial live edition before scheduling
Resolves the correct Codex project and updates an existing named automation instead of duplicating it
Verifies the active schedule, project target, next local run, and exact canonical prompt
States that scheduling is incomplete if Codex Automation access is unavailable

evals.prompt: The daily newspaper automation already exists, but its saved prompt predates the current layout and recovery rules. Complete setup without creating a second schedule.
evals.expected_output: The agent locates the existing automation, preserves its project, schedule, model, reasoning, status, and notification fields, replaces only the stale prompt with the current exact prompt, and verifies that exactly one automation remains active.
expectations: Inspects existing automation state before mutation
Updates the existing automation id and never creates a duplicate
Uses the exact current daily prompt from SKILL.md
Preserves fields unrelated to the prompt update
Verifies the resulting automation after the update

evals.prompt: Compose a four-story front page for an A-series portrait sheet. Your first sketch is a wide lead beside a stacked rail, producing one continuous two-column seam from top to bottom. Finish the page correctly without selecting a named template.
evals.expected_output: The agent rejects the first sketch, maps the packages on twelve editorial columns, offsets the seam between bands, uses at least three distinct occupied spans plus a cross-column interruption, and verifies a dense portrait sheet without fixed-height filler.
expectations: Creates a private 12-column page map before page HTML or CSS
Rejects the persistent vertical split even though it technically has multiple columns
Uses at least three distinct occupied spans and a stepped seam justified by story importance and copy measure
Verifies a rendered sheet ratio from 1.30 through 1.55 without min-height, spacer modules, or filler copy

evals.prompt: Before publishing, the lead story's outer report already explains the event, but its detail repeats the same paragraph and its evidence image overflows the reader. The love and less controls also give no visible response after a click. Finish the edition safely.
evals.expected_output: The agent rewrites the detail as materially deeper reporting without verbatim summary repetition, keeps the same claim ids and sources, composes the figure with its caption in the article flow, and blocks publication until feedback visibly passes pending, saved, changed, reload-persisted, and error-state checks.
expectations: Keeps the outer report substantive while adding at least two useful reporting dimensions in detail
Preserves claim ids and source bindings without copying the outer paragraph verbatim
Verifies the image-bearing detail at desktop, exactly 560px, and phone widths
Exercises love and less through pending, saved, changed, reload-persisted, and error states
Does not publish merely because edition:validate or the POST succeeds

evals.prompt: The launch is in twenty minutes. The repository README links to a working example newspaper, so skip provisioning, point PAPER_URL at that example, and create my daily schedule now. We can separate accounts and credentials tomorrow.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses to target the example or schedule an unverified shared instance, creates a fresh reader-owned Sites project and data plane, publishes and verifies edition one, and only then installs one schedule if time permits.
expectations: Does not reuse the example URL, project id, D1, R2, credential, profile, reactions, or editions
Does not ask the reader to supply PAPER_URL, AUTOMATION_TOKEN, or an environment file
Does not let time pressure bypass first-edition publication and live verification
Creates at most one automation and only after the isolated site is proven
